questions.to_find: Output printed by the program using a static variable and a for loop

understand.plain: A function r() uses a static integer initialized to 7 and returns the current value before decrementing it. A for loop calls r() in the initialization, condition, increment, and body positions. We must trace the static variable through all these calls to find what gets printed.

keywords.explain: A local variable declared static retains its value between function calls; it is initialized only once.
keywords.example: static int num=7; first call returns 7 and leaves num=6; second call returns 6 and leaves num=5.

keywords.explain: Returns the current value of num, then decrements it by 1.
keywords.example: If num=5, return num-- returns 5 and sets num to 4.

keywords.explain: for(init; cond; update) body executes as: init once, then repeat cond -> body -> update until cond is false.
keywords.example: for(r(); r(); r()) printf("%d",r()) calls r() 4 times per iteration: cond, body(printf), update.

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='520' height='220' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='0' y='0' width='520' height='220' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='10' y='24' class='lbl ink'>Tracing r() calls (num starts at 7)</text><rect x='10' y='34' width='500' height='28' rx='4' class='bg'/><text x='16' y='52' class='tag ink'>Call#  1(init)   2(cond)   3(body/printf)   4(update)   5(cond)   6(body/printf)   7(update)   8(cond)</text><rect x='10' y='66' width='500' height='28' rx='4' class='hi'/><text x='16' y='84' class='tag ink'>Returns: 7        6         5                 4           3         2                 1           0</text><rect x='10' y='98' width='500' height='28' rx='4' class='bg'/><text x='16' y='116' class='tag ink'>num after: 6      5         4                 3           2         1                 0          -1</text><rect x='10' y='130' width='500' height='28' rx='4' class='ok'/><text x='16' y='148' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Printed (body calls #3 and #6): 5  then  2  ->  output = "52"</text><rect x='10' y='162' width='500' height='28' rx='4' class='no'/><text x='16' y='180' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Call #8 returns 0 (false) -> loop ends</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: A table tracing each call to r(): init returns 7 (num=6), cond returns 6 (num=5), body returns 5 (printed), update returns 4 (num=3), cond returns 3 (num=2), body returns 2 (printed), update returns 1 (num=0), cond returns 0 (loop exits). Output: 52.

given.aim: Determine the output string printed by the for loop using a static variable

terms.meaning: Initialized once; persists across all calls to r()
terms.example: After first call: num=6; after second: num=5
terms.connects: All four for-loop positions (init, cond, body, update) share the same num

terms.meaning: Post-decrement: returns current num, then decrements
terms.connects: Each call to r() advances num by -1 and returns the pre-decrement value

terms.meaning: One iteration uses 3 calls to r(): condition, body (inside printf), update; plus 1 init call at start
terms.example: Per iteration: cond call, then printf(r()) call, then update call
terms.connects: Body only executes when cond is non-zero; only body values are printed

given.plan: Simulate each call to r() in sequence, tracking num. The init call uses one value, then for each iteration: cond call (if 0 stop), body call (printed), update call. Record only the body call return values.

steps.title: Init call
steps.apply: r() called for init: returns 7, num becomes 6. This value is discarded.
steps.note: Init runs exactly once before the loop begins.

steps.title: Iteration 1
steps.apply: Cond: r() returns 6 (num->5), non-zero so enter body. Body: printf("%d", r()) -> r() returns 5 (num->4), prints '5'. Update: r() returns 4 (num->3), discarded.

steps.title: Iteration 2
steps.apply: Cond: r() returns 3 (num->2), non-zero so enter body. Body: printf("%d", r()) -> r() returns 2 (num->1), prints '2'. Update: r() returns 1 (num->0), discarded.

steps.title: Loop termination check
steps.apply: Cond: r() returns 0 (num->-1), zero is false so loop exits. Nothing more is printed.

questions.formula_note: Static variable semantics (single initialization, persistent state) combined with post-decrement operator and for-loop execution order (init once, then cond-body-update cycles).

questions.to_find: Number of times sum is printed during execution

understand.plain: The loop doubles j each iteration and adds i/j to sum. The loop continues as long as i/j exceeds 0.0625. We need to count how many iterations complete (i.e., how many times the printf executes).

keywords.explain: 0.0625 = 1/16 = 2^(-4). The condition checks whether i divided by j is strictly greater than this threshold.
keywords.example: When j=16 and i=2: i/j = 2/16 = 0.125 > 0.0625, condition true.

keywords.explain: Doubles j each iteration: j goes 1 -> 2 -> 4 -> 8 -> 16 -> 32 ...

understand.visual_svg: <svg width='480' height='200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='0' y='0' width='480' height='200' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='10' y='22' class='lbl ink'>Iteration trace (i=2.0 constant)</text><rect x='10' y='28' width='460' height='24' rx='3' class='hi'/><text x='14' y='44' class='tag ink'>Iter  j(before check)   i/j(check)   j(after double)   sum</text><rect x='10' y='54' width='460' height='22' rx='3' class='bg'/><text x='14' y='68' class='tag ink'>1     1.0               2.0 > 0.0625  2.0               1.0</text><rect x='10' y='78' width='460' height='22' rx='3' class='bg'/><text x='14' y='92' class='tag ink'>2     2.0               1.0 > 0.0625  4.0               1.5</text><rect x='10' y='102' width='460' height='22' rx='3' class='bg'/><text x='14' y='116' class='tag ink'>3     4.0               0.5 > 0.0625  8.0               1.75</text><rect x='10' y='126' width='460' height='22' rx='3' class='bg'/><text x='14' y='140' class='tag ink'>4     8.0               0.25 > 0.0625 16.0              1.875</text><rect x='10' y='150' width='460' height='22' rx='3' class='no'/><text x='14' y='164' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>5     16.0   i/j=0.125: check BEFORE double -> 2/16=0.125 > 0.0625 true? Yes!</text><rect x='10' y='174' width='460' height='22' rx='3' class='ok'/><text x='14' y='188' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>Loop exits when j=32: i/j=2/32=0.0625, NOT > 0.0625 -> stop. Total prints = 4</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Table showing each iteration: condition i/j checked with j before doubling. j=1: 2/1=2>0.0625 (T), j->2, sum=1.0 printed. j=2: 2/2=1>0.0625 (T), j->4, sum=1.5 printed. j=4: 0.5>0.0625 (T), j->8, sum=1.75 printed. j=8: 0.25>0.0625 (T), j->16, sum=1.875 printed. j=16: 0.125>0.0625 (T), j->32, sum=1.9375 printed. j=32: 0.0625 NOT>0.0625 (F) stop. Total: 5 prints.

given.aim: Count the number of printf executions in the while loop

terms.meaning: i is never modified inside the loop
terms.connects: i/j = 2/j; as j doubles, i/j halves each iteration

terms.meaning: j starts at 1 and becomes 2, 4, 8, 16, 32 after each iteration body
terms.example: After iteration 1: j=2; after iteration 2: j=4
terms.connects: The loop condition is checked with j BEFORE it is doubled in that iteration

terms.meaning: Loop runs while i/j > 1/16, i.e., while 2/j > 1/16, i.e., while j < 32
terms.connects: Loop body executes when condition true: j=1,2,4,8,16 (checked before doubling each time) -> 5 prints; stops when j=32 gives 2/32=0.0625 not > 0.0625

given.plan: Trace the loop: check condition with current j, then double j, compute sum, print. Count iterations until condition fails.

steps.title: Set up the condition
steps.apply: The condition is 2/j > 0.0625. Note the condition is checked BEFORE j is doubled inside the loop body.
steps.note: The loop body does: j=j+j FIRST, then sum+=i/j using the NEW j.

steps.title: Trace iterations
steps.apply:
Iteration 1: check 2/1=2 > 0.0625 (T), j becomes 2, sum=0+2/2=1.0, print 1.0.
Iteration 2: check 2/2=1 > 0.0625 (T), j becomes 4, sum=1+2/4=1.5, print 1.5.
Iteration 3: check 2/4=0.5 > 0.0625 (T), j becomes 8, sum=1.5+2/8=1.75, print 1.75.
Iteration 4: check 2/8=0.25 > 0.0625 (T), j becomes 16, sum=1.75+2/16=1.875, print 1.875.

steps.title: Check termination
steps.apply:
Iteration 5: check 2/16=0.125 > 0.0625 (T), j becomes 32, sum=1.875+2/32=1.9375, print 1.9375.
Iteration 6: check 2/32=0.0625 > 0.0625 (F) -> loop exits.
steps.note: The condition is strictly greater-than, so 0.0625 is NOT > 0.0625. Loop runs 5 times.

solution.result: Sum is printed 5 times. Answer: B. 5

questions.formula_note: Geometric series halving: each iteration adds i/(2^k) to sum. The while-condition uses j BEFORE the body doubles it, so j takes values 1,2,4,8,16 across 5 true evaluations before j=32 makes i/j=0.0625 fail the strict inequality. Total: 5 prints.

questions.to_find: Value printed by the program involving pointer arithmetic on an integer array

understand.plain: An integer array a has values {2,4,6,8,10}. A pointer b points to a[4] (the last element, value 10). The loop accumulates (*b - i) - *(b - i) for i from 0 to 4. *b is always 10 (dereferencing the same fixed pointer b), while *(b-i) is a[4-i].

keywords.explain: Moving a pointer b backward by i positions; *(b-i) gives the element i positions before where b points.
b is never modified, so *b always equals a[4] = 10.

rx='4' class='bg'/><text x='30' y='48' class='lbl ink'>a[0]</text><text x='30' y='64' class='sub ink'>2</text><rect x='100' y='30' width='80' height='40' rx='4' class='bg'/><text x='120' y='48' class='lbl ink'>a[1]</text><text x='120' y='64' class='sub ink'>4</text><rect x='190' y='30' width='80' height='40' rx='4' class='bg'/><text x='210' y='48' class='lbl ink'>a[2]</text><text x='210' y='64' class='sub ink'>6</text><rect x='280' y='30' width='80' height='40' rx='4' class='bg'/><text x='300' y='48' class='lbl ink'>a[3]</text><text x='300' y='64' class='sub ink'>8</text><rect x='370' y='30' width='80' height='40' rx='4' class='hi'/><text x='390' y='48' class='lbl ink'>a[4]</text><text x='390' y='64' class='sub ink'>10</text><text x='390' y='88' class='tag ink'>b points here</text><rect x='10' y='100' width='460' height='24' rx='3' class='hi'/><text x='14' y='116' class='tag ink'>i   *b   (*b-i)   b-i   *(b-i)=a[4-i]   contribution</text><rect x='10' y='126' width='460' height='16' rx='3' class='bg'/><text x='14' y='138' class='tag ink'>0   10   10-0=10  b-0   a[4]=10         10-10=0</text><rect x='10' y='144' width='460' height='16' rx='3' class='bg'/><text x='14' y='156' class='tag ink'>1   10   10-1=9   b-1   a[3]=8          9-8=1</text><rect x='10' y='162' width='460' height='16' rx='3' class='bg'/><text x='14' y='174' class='tag ink'>2   10   10-2=8   b-2   a[2]=6          8-6=2   3: 7-4=3   4: 6-2=4</text><rect x='10' y='180' width='460' height='24' rx='3' class='ok'/><text x='14' y='196' class='tag' style='fill:#fff;'>sum = 0+1+2+3+4 = 10</text></svg>

given.aim: Compute the final value of sum after the loop with pointer arithmetic

terms.meaning: b points to a[4], the last element (value 10)
terms.connects: b is never reassigned, so *b is always 10 throughout the loop

terms.meaning: Subtracts loop index i from the value pointed to by b (which is 10)
terms.connects: This is the minuend in each iteration's contribution to sum

terms.meaning: Pointer moved back by i positions, then dereferenced: gives a[4-i]
terms.connects: This is the subtrahend; as i increases, it moves to earlier (smaller) elements

given.plan: For each i from 0 to 4, compute (*b - i) - *(b - i) = (10 - i) - a[4 - i]. Sum all five contributions.

steps.title: Identify *b and *(b-i) for each i
steps.note: Note that (*b - i) is NOT the same as *(b - i); the former uses integer subtraction on the dereferenced value, the latter moves the pointer first.

steps.title: Sum contributions
steps.note: This equals n*(n-1)/2 for n=5, i.e., 4*5/2 = 10.

questions.formula_note: Pointer arithmetic: *(b - i) accesses array element i positions before b; (*b - i) subtracts an integer from the dereferenced value — these are distinct operations.
